scenario: Catalyst SaaS, a publisher handling 80000 events per day, integrates Slack with a Camunda 8.8 HTTP Webhook connector. Slack performs a one-time verification challenge by POSTing {"type":"url_verification","challenge":"abc"} and expects the body {"challenge":"abc"} returned with HTTP 200 in under 3 seconds; otherwise normal process correlation must run.
question: Which Verification expression handles the Slack challenge without creating a process instance?

options.text: = if request.body.type = "url_verification" then { body: { challenge: request.body.challenge }, statusCode: 200 } else null so the webhook returns the challenge and skips correlation
= if request.body.type = "url_verification" then { challenge: request.body.challenge } else null so the connector returns the raw body and still triggers a process instance for each call
Configure an Activation Condition of = request.body.type != "url_verification" so verification calls are silently dropped without any HTTP response to Slack
Set a Response Expression instead of a Verification Expression so the webhook continues normal correlation while also returning the challenge body to Slack

a.text: Correct. When the verification expression returns a non-null value, the webhook returns that response immediately and skips activation, HMAC, and correlation.

b.text: Incorrect. Without wrapping in a body object and an explicit statusCode the response format is incomplete, and correlation would still run on the verification call.

c.text: Incorrect. Activation Condition drops the call from process correlation but does not return a custom HTTP response to the caller, which Slack requires.

d.text: Incorrect. Response Expression runs after correlation completes; the verification step must run before correlation is attempted at all.

explanation: The Verification Expression returns a response object (body, statusCode, headers) and short-circuits the entire webhook pipeline so no process instance is created. Returning null falls through to normal processing.
